title: Justice Is Blind

jokes.text: Well, I didn't read it. I just got it yesterday.
jokes.setup: Barry confidently negotiates a plea bargain, saying they'll never get an offer this good again
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hasn't even read the offer he's recommending
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Barry's incompetence through confident delivery of absurd contradiction

jokes.text: F.Y.I., I'm trying to get back into the dating world.
jokes.setup: Family is discussing serious legal matter
jokes.punchline: Barry randomly announces his dating life as if it's relevant
jokes.explanation: Classic Barry self-centeredness, but fairly predictable for his character

jokes.text: Hey, you're not one of those silly men that's dressed like a woman, are you?
jokes.setup: Woman meeting Barry for a date
jokes.punchline: Her immediate concern about Barry's gender presentation
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny setup for Barry's dating incompetence

jokes.text: No, baby. I'm the real thing.
jokes.setup: Woman asks if he's a man dressed as a woman
jokes.punchline: Barry's overly confident masculine response
jokes.explanation: Decent Barry moment but nothing exceptional

jokes.text: Well, you know, God doesn't care how big your teeth are.
jokes.setup: Cindi says 'I'm a mole' meaning undercover agent
jokes.punchline: George Sr. thinks she's talking about her physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that shows George Sr.'s obliviousness while creating dramatic irony

jokes.text: You could grind off about... I don't know... 30%, maybe more. Yeah, I wouldn't miss it.
jokes.setup: Continuing to think Cindi is self-conscious about her teeth
jokes.punchline: George Sr. gives specific percentage of teeth to remove
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding with George Sr.'s characteristically blunt advice

jokes.text: Is that a shot at me? 'Cause that makes me want to read it less.
jokes.setup: Michael points out they don't know what the plea is yet
jokes.punchline: Barry takes it as personal criticism and responds with spite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry logic - doubling down on incompetence when called out

jokes.text: He thinks he's a lawyer 'cause he played one in 10th grade.
jokes.setup: Michael offers to read the legal document
jokes.punchline: Lucille's dismissive comment about his credentials
jokes.explanation: Solid Lucille putdown setting up the flashback

jokes.text: The Trial of Captain Hook
jokes.setup: Reference to Michael's dramatic experience
jokes.punchline: The play was an original work about Captain Hook's legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Absurd title that connects to maritime law theme, rewards close attention

jokes.text: Well, you left during my solo.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims not to remember the play
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals she left during his performance
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing family dysfunction through mundane details

jokes.text: considering she was repeating the ninth grade at the time
jokes.setup: Michael defending his memory against Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Reveals Lindsay was held back in school
jokes.explanation: Solid family insult that establishes character history

jokes.text: I will pack your sweet pink mouth with so much ice cream... you'll be the envy of every Jerry and Jane on the block.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to motivate a tutor for Maeby
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate, vaguely sexual ice cream metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: We're the only house on the block.
jokes.setup: Tobias talks about being envied by neighbors
jokes.punchline: Tutor points out there are no other neighbors
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Tobias's grandiosity with simple factual correction

jokes.text: More than five?
jokes.setup: Gob challenging Michael about his sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Gob raising the stakes when Michael claims more than four women
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat but fairly standard sibling competition

jokes.text: Let's say 'five'.
jokes.setup: Gob pressuring Michael about his sexual history
jokes.punchline: Michael's awkward compromise to end the conversation
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's discomfort with personal topics through deflection

jokes.text: These are lawyers... that's Latin for 'liar.'
jokes.setup: Gob encouraging Michael to lie to women at the bar
jokes.punchline: False etymology connecting 'lawyer' to 'liar'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Gob's pseudo-intellectual character

jokes.text: How can something be 'no less than three' if it exceeds six? I mean, six is still more than three, right?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to parse confusing legal language
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about basic mathematical logic in legal speak
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Michael's distraction and legal document confusion

jokes.text: If you're pleading with me to have a drink with you, you can stop begging.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions working on a plea
jokes.punchline: Maggie misinterprets 'plea' as pleading for her attention
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that introduces Maggie's confidence and sets up their meeting

jokes.text: I'm Maggie Lizer, as in 'Maggie lies her ass off.' One person laughed at that once, and I don't know why I keep trying it.
jokes.setup: Maggie introducing herself with a prepared joke
jokes.punchline: Her admission that the joke doesn't work but she keeps using it
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor that reveals character while being genuinely funny about unfunny jokes

jokes.text: Maritime. Maritime law. Lawyers of the sea.
jokes.setup: Maggie asks what kind of law he practices
jokes.punchline: Michael's improvised fake specialty with grandiose description
jokes.explanation: Good setup for maritime law running gag, shows Michael's awkward lying

jokes.text: Chareth... Cutestory?
jokes.setup: Michael giving a fake name
jokes.punchline: The absurd combination creating 'Cute story'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that becomes a memorable running element, perfect blend of stupid and clever

jokes.text: You got all the answers wrong. You know you, you even got your name wrong here. It says, 'S. Fünke.'
jokes.setup: Tutor looking at Maeby's test
jokes.punchline: Reveals she somehow got her own name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the depth of Maeby's academic problems

jokes.text: what they want you to do is put an 'S' if you're single, an 'M' if you're married. I mean, most of us are all single
jokes.setup: Explaining the S on her test paper
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong explanation of a simple name field
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby logic - confidently wrong with a touch of worldly cynicism

jokes.text: Oh, there... there's six 20s here. - That's right.
jokes.setup: Tutor counting money Maeby gave him
jokes.punchline: Maeby confirming wrong math while supposedly needing math tutoring
jokes.explanation: Subtle irony that Maeby can't do math but calculated the tutor's payment wrong

jokes.text: How else am I gonna get to work? - You ride a dog to work?
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Maggie having a dog
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal interpretation of her transportation comment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that sets up Michael's obliviousness to her blindness

jokes.text: Well, it's tough with your hands in the way.
jokes.setup: Maggie asks to see his smile
jokes.punchline: Michael commenting on her hands blocking her view, not realizing she's blind
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience realizes Michael doesn't know she's blind

jokes.text: Uh, well, I have an irreversible case of ocular retinoblastoma. - Hey. I thought we said no more law talk.
jokes.setup: Maggie trying to tell Michael something personal about her medical condition
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks medical terminology is legal jargon
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Michael's complete misunderstanding of her condition

jokes.text: I really can't see anybody right now. - Oh, well, I can't see anybody ever. - I'm blind. - I'm wasted.
jokes.setup: Two characters using 'see' in different contexts
jokes.punchline: Parallel admissions with completely different meanings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel structure with perfect economy - reveals her blindness while he remains oblivious

jokes.text: Come on. I took a blind woman home with no intention of dating her again? Please!
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he was with a blind woman
jokes.punchline: His moral outrage at his own hypothetical behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's guilt overriding his original intentions

jokes.text: It looks like you're tweaking her nipples through a chain-link fence. - Yep. Yeah, that's it.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claiming his interaction with Cindi isn't what it looks like
jokes.punchline: Lucille's specific description followed by George Sr.'s matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd visual description with George Sr.'s shameless admission

jokes.text: So you could run off with this great redwood of a whore?
jokes.setup: Lucille confronting George Sr. about his motives
jokes.punchline: Her creative tree-based insult for Cindi
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lucille insult combining size reference with elegant phrasing

jokes.text: a major blonde who just majored in marine biology, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Gob bragging about his romantic conquest
jokes.punchline: His weak attempt at innuendo about marine biology
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob sexual boasting with maritime connection, but fairly weak wordplay

jokes.text: I don't know what you mean. I can't imagine what that means.
jokes.setup: Gob's poor attempt at sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Michael's complete bewilderment at the nonsensical connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to Gob's failed wordplay

jokes.text: What's wrong with her? / Nothing's wrong with her. / She's blind.
jokes.setup: Gob assuming something must be wrong with Michael's date
jokes.punchline: Michael's defensive response followed by the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff with Michael's protective instinct

jokes.text: I'm Chareth Cutestory, a pirate lawyer.
jokes.setup: Michael recounting his fake identity to Gob
jokes.punchline: Adding 'pirate' to the already absurd maritime lawyer persona
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the absurdity while staying in character theme

jokes.text: You've just won the gold medal at the sexual Special Olympics!
jokes.setup: Gob encouraging Michael to take advantage of Maggie's blindness
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate Olympics metaphor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Gob's moral bankruptcy, but relies on cheap shock value

jokes.text: Well, she was a darer. She's one of those girls who just dares you to do things. - You married her? - I needed a dare!
jokes.setup: Gob explaining how he got married
jokes.punchline: His childish justification for a life-changing decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - treating marriage like a playground challenge

jokes.text: Oh, wait! What time is it? Oh, God! 'Seal the deal.' My seal deal! I gotta get to Sea Land.
jokes.setup: Wife suggesting they consummate the marriage
jokes.punchline: Wordplay reveals she literally sells seals, not sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that recontextualizes the entire conversation through misdirection

jokes.text: God! Do you ever listen to what I say? I'm sorry I don't memorize every word that comes out of your mouth.
jokes.setup: Wife frustrated that Gob doesn't know about her seal business
jokes.punchline: Gob's defensive response reveals his self-centeredness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing why Gob's relationships fail

jokes.text: Boy, the lengths you'll go to sleep with a woman. Believe me, we didn't do any sleeping.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Gob's marriage
jokes.punchline: Gob's false bravado about sexual activity that didn't happen
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual wordplay with dramatic irony that Gob is lying

jokes.text: So you didn't get a case of 'I just had a one-night stand with a blind girl'-itus?
jokes.setup: Maggie checking if Michael feels guilty
jokes.punchline: Her medical-sounding name for guilt about sleeping with a blind person
jokes.explanation: Clever medical wordplay that shows Maggie's awareness of potential guilt

jokes.text: three years is what I give them and then one of them dies and the other one is not too far behind.
jokes.setup: Maggie asking Michael to describe an elderly couple in the park
jokes.punchline: Michael's morbid prediction while supposedly describing beauty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts the romantic moment while showing Michael's distraction

jokes.text: I can smell right through you, Chareth. You're reading.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to multitask romance and work
jokes.punchline: Maggie catching him through her enhanced senses
jokes.explanation: Good use of her blindness as character ability rather than disability

jokes.text: Well, you know, I almost did strangle myself on my own prayer shawl. A self-choking for the ages.
jokes.setup: George Sr. discussing blooper footage from his wisdom videos
jokes.punchline: His proud description of a near-death mishap
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits George Sr.'s ego and faux-spiritual character

jokes.text: Faith is not a fact! - Oh, yes, it is. You said so yourself in Caged Wisdom.
jokes.setup: George Sr. frustrated with Cindi's faith-based reasoning
jokes.punchline: Cindi using his own teachings against him
jokes.explanation: Good irony as George Sr.'s fake wisdom comes back to haunt him

jokes.text: Faith is a fact. No. Faith is a facet. I almost said faith is a fact.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining the blooper reference
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s mispronunciation created the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that explains the entire misunderstanding through simple mispronunciation

jokes.text: I am going to trial because you don't understand what a blooper reel is?
jokes.setup: George Sr. realizing his predicament
jokes.punchline: The absurd stakes of the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how small misunderstandings have huge consequences

jokes.text: You can only get an annulment if you haven't consummated the marriage first. - Well, I guess I'll just stay married then... because we all know that I closed that deal, right?
jokes.setup: Barry explaining annulment requirements
jokes.punchline: Gob's false bravado about sexual performance we know didn't happen
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience knows Gob is lying about consummation

jokes.text: I couldn't breast-feed any of you kids because of that man!
jokes.setup: Lucille complaining about George Sr.'s tweaking behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals the long-term consequences of his actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille oversharing with unexpected historical revelation

jokes.text: They still look fabulous.
jokes.setup: Lucille's revelation about breast-feeding problems
jokes.punchline: Buster's inappropriate compliment about his mother's breasts
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster inappropriate comment that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: Even when you slept with my sister, it was for a good reason. Got her to stop drinking, didn't it?
jokes.setup: Lucille forgiving George Sr.'s infidelity with the informant
jokes.punchline: Reveals previous affair had beneficial consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark family history revealed casually with twisted logic

jokes.text: We've got Lizer.
jokes.setup: Seeing Maggie as the prosecutor
jokes.punchline: Barry's reaction echoing Maggie's own name joke
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier name joke

jokes.text: What are you hiding from? You've got to call this off. You gotta get more time. Get more time. She can smell me.
jokes.setup: Michael panicking in court upon seeing Maggie
jokes.punchline: His paranoid belief that she can detect him by smell
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's paranoia about being caught

jokes.text: That's the blind chick you slept with?
jokes.setup: Gob realizing who the prosecutor is
jokes.punchline: His characteristically blunt and inappropriate observation
jokes.explanation: Standard Gob inappropriate comment, nothing exceptional

jokes.text: The solution to all our problems is staring you right in the face and it can't even see you.
jokes.setup: Family encouraging Michael to manipulate Maggie
jokes.punchline: Visual pun about blindness making the solution 'visible' but not to her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - visual, ironic, and character-specific. Near-perfect economy.

jokes.text: Please welcome... Surely Fünke!
jokes.setup: George Michael at a fund-raiser for a sick student
jokes.punchline: Reveals Maeby is using 'Surely' as her fake sick identity
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that connects earlier confusion to elaborate scheme

jokes.text: What about you? Don't tell me another pirate is still roaming the high seas.
jokes.setup: Maggie asking about Michael's work
jokes.punchline: Maritime-themed continuation of their role-play
jokes.explanation: Decent callback maintaining their established dynamic

jokes.text: Three of the seals died on their way to Chad.
jokes.setup: Wife explaining her seal business problems
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of animal deaths in business context
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about animal welfare treated as business problem

tmdb_overview: Gob steals a file on the government's case against the Bluth Company that Maggie had given to Michael. Tobias is sent to Maggie's house to get evidence, George Michael tries to prove Maeby's fraud, and Lindsay fights to remove the Ten Commandments from the courthouse lawn.
insight: Legal system confusion fuels 58 jokes at 2.06-per-minute clip.
